questions.q: The Sargasso Sea is a part of the

options.E: None of the above/ more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Sargasso sea is located in the North-Atlantic Ocean, it got its name from the Algae species 'Sargassum'. Four different currents surround this sea. Gulf Stream (warm) in the west, North Atlantic Current (Warm) in the North, Canary Current (cold) in the east and North Atlantic Equatorial (warm) current in the South. This sea has no coastline. The seaweed Sargassum here provides food to Turtles, shrimps, crabs, etc, hence ecologically it is rich but this seaweed sometimes creates problems in the navigation of ships.

questions.q: What is the boundary line between India and China called?

options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Sir Henry McMahon was the British negotiator. The line was negotiated after the Shimla conference in 1914. It was negotiated between Tibet and British India. China was not a part of this treaty and considers this line as illegal. During the 1950s, China occupied 'Aksai Chin' which led to the war in 1962, the boundary that came after the war is known as the Line of Actual Control (LAC) between India and China. After the first Anglo-Burma war (1824-26), the British annexed Burma after signing the Treaty of Yandabo in 1826. Durand Line is between Afghanistan and Pakistan named after Mortimer Durand. Indira Point is the southernmost point of India, located on Nicobar Island, earlier it was known as Pygmalion Point or Parsons Point. Radcliffe Line is named after the chairman of the Border Commission 'Cyril Radcliffe' is the line between India and Pakistan.

questions.q: Sunda trench lies parallel to the island of

options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Sunda Trench, also known as the 'Java Trench', is an oceanic trench located in the Indian Ocean near Sumatra. It was formed by the subduction of the Australian-Capricorn plate under the Eurasian Plate. The trench lies parallel to the island of Sumatra.

questions.q: Which of the following countries does not have a border with the dead sea?

options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The dead sea is also called the salt sea. It is also known as the Arabic Lake. The sea is located between Jordan to the east and Israel to the west. It also shares a border with Palestine.

questions.q: The largest island of Japan in terms of geographical area is

options.E: None of the above /more than one

questions.explanation: Four main islands of Japan are, Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku (decreasing order area-wise). Japan is surrounded by the Pacific Ocean, the Sea of Okhotsk, the Sea of Japan, and the East China Sea. Honshu is the largest and most populated island in Japan. Tokyo is situated on this island. It is a mountainous island and home to many of the country's active volcanoes. Its most famous peak is Mt. Fuji (active volcano) is the highest peak in Japan. Important industries of Japan: Tokyo- electrical industries; Yokohama-precious metal industries; Nagoya-Automobile industries.

questions.q: Greenland is a part of

options.E: None of the above /more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Greenland is the largest island in the world and has the lowest population density. It is geographically located in North America but politically located in Europe (Denmark). New Guinea is the 2nd largest Island in the world. Borneo Island is the 3rd largest Island and the only Island in the world that comprises three countries: Indonesia (73%), Malaysia (26%) and Brunei (1%).

questions.q: Among the following continents, which one has the highest number of countries?

options.E: None of the above /more than one

questions.explanation: The African continent has the highest number of countries in the world. It has a total of 54 countries. The highest peak in Africa is Mt. Kilimanjaro (a dormant volcano), which is in Tanzania and the lowest point in Africa is Lake Assal (-155m) which is in Djibouti. The longest or largest mountain range in Africa is the Atlas Mountain range which is situated in Algeria, Tunisia, and Morocco (trick-ATM) and the highest peak of the Atlas range is Mt. Toubkal (Morocco). Africa is also known as 'The Island of Plateaus' and is a dark continent. Algeria is the largest country area-wise while population-wise is Nigeria and Seychelles is the smallest country area-wise. The Tropic of Cancer, the Tropic of Capricorn, the Equator, and the Prime Meridian line all pass through Africa. Europe has 44 countries. The Arctic Circle passes through Finland, Norway, Sweden, and Russia. Area wise Russia is the largest country in Europe as well as in the world and Vatican City is the smallest country in Europe as well as in the World. Mount Elbrus is the highest peak of the Caucasus Mountain and the highest point in Europe. Asia has 48 countries. The highest point is Mount Everest while the lowest is the Dead Sea. North America lies entirely within the northern hemisphere and almost all within the western hemisphere. Mt Denali (Mount McKinley-6190 m) in Alaska is the highest point while the Bad water basin in Death Valley (86m) in California is its lowest point.

questions.q: The Arctic Region and the Antarctic continent are situated near

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The Arctic is the northernmost region on Earth. The Arctic is dominated by the Arctic Ocean basin, and the icy reaches of Scandinavia, Russia, the U.S. state of Alaska, Canada, and Greenland. Antarctica is the world's southernmost and fifth-largest continent. Its landmass is almost wholly covered by a vast ice sheet.

questions.q: Addu Atoll is situated in which ocean?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: An atoll is a ring-shaped coral reef island. It generally surrounds a body of water called a lagoon. The Addu Atoll Biosphere Reserve is located north of the Chagos Archipelago and is the southernmost atoll of the Maldives. It is located in the Indian Ocean. The Great Chagos Bank in the Indian Ocean is the largest atoll in the world. Recently India and Maldives signed agreements to develop the 'Addu Tourism Zone'.

questions.q: Niagara Falls in North America is located between

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Niagara Falls in North America is located between Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. A group of five glacial freshwater lakes spread in the US and Canada are known as the Great Lakes. Lake Michigan: Located entirely in the USA. Lake Superior: Largest freshwater lake (in surface area). Lake Huron and Lake Superior are connected by the Soo Canal/Soo Lock. Lake Ontario: Smallest Lake among the great lakes.

questions.q: Which one of the following lakes of West Africa has become dry and turned into desert?
questions.explanation: Lake Faguibine is an isolated lake in Mali, west of Timbuktu (Tombouctou). It lies north of the Niger River in the Macina Depression. Lake Faguibine in northern Mali is dry and has been since the 1970s. Over seven years, droughts in the 1970s dried up the lakes. Then sand filled the channels connecting the lakes to the river Niger, with the result that when rain finally returned the water could no longer reach the lakes.

questions.q: One of the following pairs is a mismatch. Find it.

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: People of the Maasai ethnic group mainly dwell in Kenya and Tanzania which form the Central Eastern part of Africa. Sakai are the aboriginal tribes of Malaysia and Indonesia. The Bedouin, Beduin, or Bedu are nomadic Arab tribes who have historically inhabited the desert regions in the Arabian Peninsula and North Africa. The Kyrgyz ethnic group is native to Central Asia, primarily Kyrgyzstan. Hence, all of the above are correct.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a coalfield of Germany?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Lorraine Basin is located in France. It is famous for the Iron and Steel Industry. Ruhr Valley of Germany is famous for coal and Iron ore deposits.

questions.q: Name the city of Russia that is known as 'Venice of North'

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Venice is a city in Italy and is often called the Queen of the Adriatic. It is called the city of dreams or the city of cities/cities of bridges. Venice is made up of many islands (more than 100), hence it is also called a floating city. St. Petersburg is known as the 'Venice of North' as it is made up of more than 42 islands in the delta of the Neva River.

questions.q: Pyrenees mountain ranges separate the countries?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Pyrenees Mountains are a chain of mountains in Europe that create the border between France and Spain. The highest peak in the Pyrenees Mountains is Pico de Aneto in the Spanish Pyrenees, with an elevation of 3,404 m (11,168 feet). These mountain ranges are famous for granite, iron ore and limestone. Note: Maginot line separates France and Germany while the Hindenburg line separates Belgium and Germany.

questions.q: Which one of the following countries is the largest country without borders in terms of geographical area?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Japan is a group of four Islands i.e. Hokkaido, Honshu, Shikoku and Kyushu in East Asia. It is situated in the northwest Pacific Ocean and is bordered on the west by the Sea of Japan while extending from the Sea of Okhotsk in the north toward the East China Sea, the Philippine Sea, and Taiwan in the south. Honshu, historically called Hondo, is the largest and most populous island of Japan followed by Hokkaido. Japan's capital Tokyo is on Honshu Island. Shikoku is the smallest island in Japan.

questions.q: The country which has the longest North-South (latitudinal) extension of its territory is

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Chile has the longest North-south (latitude) extension of its territory. Note: BPSC has answered Russia in its official answer key.

questions.q: Which one of the following countries has the highest number of islands?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Indonesia has the highest number of islands. The Group of Islands is called Archipelago. The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia. It is situated in the western Pacific Ocean and consists of around 7,641 islands. Manila is the capital of the Philippines. The Ramon Magsaysay Award is given by the Philippines government. It is often called the 'Nobel Peace Prize of Asia'. The award is named after Ramon Magsaysay, the seventh president of the Republic of the Philippines after World War II. Maldives: Officially the Republic of Maldives, is an archipelagic state located in South Asia, situated in the Indian Ocean. It lies southwest of Sri Lanka and India. Male is the capital of Maldives. Maldives is the smallest country in Asia (area-wise) and is often called the 'country of thousand corals'.

questions.q: Among the following countries, which one is the largest producer of Saffron in the world?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Iran is the largest producer of Saffron in the world. Saffron in India is generally produced in the Karewa soil of Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.q: Which one of the following countries is the largest producer of Uranium in the world?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Largest producer of Uranium in the world is Kazakhstan but Australia has the largest reserve of Uranium in the world. Australia tops in the production of Iron Ore and Bauxite. Australia has the largest reserves of Gold, Lead and Uranium in the world.

questions.q: Which of the following countries has the largest reserve of coal deposits?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The USA has the largest reserve of coal deposits. The Appalachian coal region is the famous coal region of America. Bihar has coal blocks located in the kahalgaon region of Bhagalpur.

questions.q: Which of the following is not associated with NAFTA?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) was an agreement signed by Canada, Mexico, and the United States that created a trilateral trade bloc in North America. The agreement came into force on 1st January 1994 and superseded the 1988 Canada-United States Free Trade Agreement between the United States and Canada which is now replaced by United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) or NAFTA 2.0.

questions.q: Which one of the following is a landlocked country?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Hungary (Capital: Budapest) is a landlocked country in Central Europe and part of the Carpathian Basin. It is bordered by Slovakia to the north, Ukraine to the northeast, Romania to the east and southeast, Serbia to the south, Croatia and Slovenia to the southwest, and Austria to the west. It has the two longest rivers called Danube and Tisza which divide Hungary into three parts. The Hungarian parliament has elected Katalin Novak, as the first-ever female president. Belgium (Kingdom of Belgium), is a country in Northwestern Europe. The capital and largest city is Brussels. Note: The headquarters of NATO is in Brussels, Belgium.

questions.q: Which one among the following is the largest island in the area?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Borneo Island is the 3rd largest Island in the world and Asia's largest, the only Island in the world which shares a boundary with Indonesia (73% area), Brunei (26% area), and Malaysia (1% area). World's largest Island is Greenland (Three times larger than Borneo) followed by New Guinea, Borneo and Madagascar (the Largest Island of the Indian Ocean). Sumatra is one of the Sunda Islands (a group of Islands in the Malay Archipelago) of Western Indonesia. Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago. Great Britain is an island in the North Atlantic Ocean on the northwest coast of Europe.

questions.q: In which of the following areas, does the Mediterranean climate not prevail?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Mediterranean climate lies between 30 degrees north to 45 degrees N and 30 degrees S to 45 degrees S of the equator. For fruit production, Mediterranean forests are called the 'Orchards of the World'. Mediterranean regions of the world: The area around the Mediterranean Sea, California in the USA, Central Chile, Cape Province of South Africa, South West Australia.

questions.q: The country, which is the largest silk producer in the world, is

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: China is the largest silk producer in the world followed by India then Uzbekistan. In India, Karnataka is the largest silk producer state. In India, four types of silk are produced namely: mulberry, tsar, Muga, and Eri. Jharkhand produces around 80% of the total tsar in India. Karnataka leads in mulberry production, while Assam leads in Muga and Eri production. Brazil is the world's leading producer of coffee. Bhagalpur is known as 'silk city' of Bihar.

questions.q: During 10000 BC, the population of the world was

options.E: None of these/More than one of these

questions.explanation: By 10,000 B.C., the world's population was around 1 million. This was the period of transition from the Palaeolithic to the Mesolithic period. Progressive change in life forms in the form of domestication of animals, more apt design of hunting tools, etc. started coming into the picture. The world reached its 1st billion Population by 1804. Notably, the second billion took just a little more than a century after 1804.

questions.q: Cocos Plate lies between

options.B: South America and the Pacific Plate
options.C: The Red Sea and the Persian Gulf
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Lying between Central America and the Pacific Plate, the Cocos Plate is named after Cocos Island which rides upon it. Cocos plate subduction under the Caribbean plate began at the end of the Tertiary, provoking a generalized orogeny accompanied by strong earthquakes and volcanism. (Orogeny means the formation of the mountain range by compression and uplifting of the earth's crust). It is a small-sized, triangular-shaped young oceanic tectonic plate. 7 minor plates: Arabian Plate, Caribbean Plate, Cocos Plate, Juan de Fuca Plate, Nazca Plate, Philippine Sea Plate, and the Scotia Plate.

questions.q: 'Rust Bowl' of the USA is associated with which one of the following regions?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Rust Bowl or Rust Belt is a geographic region that was formerly a manufacturing or industrial powerhouse but is now in irreversible decline. It can occur anywhere globally but is most commonly used to refer to the U.S. Northeast and Midwest. New York, Indiana, Pittsburgh, etc. are considered a part of the rust belt. The Great Lakes region contains New York among others. So, 'E' becomes the right choice.

questions.q: Which one of the following is known as the 'Coffee Port' of the world?

options.E: None of these/More than one of these

questions.explanation: 'Santos' is considered the 'Coffee port' of the world. It is in Brazil. Brazil is the largest producer of coffee in the world. Santos is a port in the state of Sao Paulo of Brazil. Large coffee plantation in Brazil is called Fazenda. Brazil is the only country through which both the Tropic of Capricorn and the Equator pass.

questions.q: What is the name of mid-latitude grassland in South America?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Pampas grasslands are in South America (Argentina, Brazil, and Uruguay). Grasslands are in the intermediate stage of ecological succession. They occupy around 20% of the total land surface. Grasslands present in the Tropics are often called 'Savannas'. Indian grasslands are primarily Savanna-type. Grasslands present in the temperate belts are known by different names in different countries: Prairies in the 'USA', Steppes in 'Russia', Downs in 'Australia', Puszta in 'Hungary', Veld - It is a flat area covered with grass or low scrub, especially found in the countries of South Africa (Lesotho, Eswatini, Zimbabwe, and Botswana). Note: Grasses present in the Temperate region are short in comparison to tropical grasslands but are more nutritious hence, we find a more productive dairy system in the temperate belt.

questions.q: Which one of the following is the characteristic climate of the Tropical Savannah Region?

options.D: A Definite Dry and Wet Season

questions.explanation: According to World Atlas, the tropical savanna climate is also called the tropical wet and dry climate. It is the climate experienced in savanna or tropical grassland regions of the world. These places are located near the equator, and they lie between the Southern and the Northern Tropics. The climate dominates many parts of the African continent, the northern region of South America, and parts of Asia such as India. The tropical savanna climate has alternating dry and wet seasons, hence its name. It shares some similar characteristics with the tropical monsoon climate, but it receives less annual rainfall as compared to the tropical monsoon climate. In the Koppen classification, the climate is designated as Aw or As.

questions.q: The Great Victoria Desert is located in:

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Great Victoria Desert is located in Australia. The Great Victoria Desert is the world's prime spot for reptiles. Australia is often called the 'Land of Thirsty Continent'. Its central and western parts are deserts hence we find all the important cities on its coast. The main deserts of Australia are the Sandy Desert, The Great Victoria Desert (the largest desert of Australia), and Gibson, Simpson, and Tanami. Most of the mines in Australia are present on the western side of the continent. In India: The 'Thar Desert' is present. It is the most populated desert in the world. North Africa: The 'Sahara Desert' is located here. It is the world's largest hot desert. 'Sirocco' are hot winds that blow in the Sahara Desert. Top 5 Largest Deserts in The World: 1. Antarctic Polar Desert. 2. Arctic Polar Desert. 3. Sahara Desert. 4. Arabian Desert. 5. Gobi Desert.

questions.q: Which of the following activities is performed at Coolgardie?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Coolgardie is famous for Gold Mining in Australia. Kalgoorlie is also a Gold mine. Both are located in Western Australia. Australia has the largest reserves of gold in the world. Australia is the leading producer of Iron ore and has the largest reserves in the world. The largest gold reserve in India is in Karnataka. In Bihar, Karmatia village of Jamui district is known for its gold deposits. Some notable Gold Fields in India are Kolar gold fields and Hutti gold fields in Karnataka.

questions.q: Which of the following seas has the highest average salinity?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: According to the option, the highest salinity is in the Dead Sea but overall the highest salinity is in Lake Van Turkey. The Dead Sea is a landlocked sea located between Israel and Jordan. Black Sea bordering countries: Ukraine, Russia, Georgia, Turkey, Bulgaria, and Romania. The Yellow Sea bordering countries: China, North Korea, and South Korea. Mediterranean Sea - It is an intercontinental sea between Asia, Africa, and Europe. The Nile River drains to the Mediterranean Sea. 21 different countries have coastlines on the Mediterranean Sea. Highest salinity in water bodies: Lake Van in Turkey (330 per mille), Dead Sea (238 per mille), Great Salt Lake (220 per mille).

questions.q: The Mediterranean Sea is a border of which of the following countries? 1. Jordan  2. Iraq  3. Lebanon  4. Syria  Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The countries with coastlines on the Mediterranean Sea are Albania, Algeria, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia, Cyprus, Egypt, France, Greece, Israel, Italy, Lebanon, Libya, Malta, Morocco, Monaco, Montenegro, Slovenia, Spain, Syria, Tunisia and Turkey. In addition, the Gaza Strip and the British Overseas Territories of Gibraltar and Akrotiri and Dhekelia have coastlines on the sea. On the Eastern shore, Turkey, Syria, Lebanon and Israel have their coastline on the Mediterranean Sea.

questions.q: Which of the following has/have shrunk immensely/dried up in the recent past due to human activities? 1. Aral Sea  2. Black Sea  3. Lake Baikal  Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: As per NASA, over the last 50 years, the Aral Sea has shrunk by about 75% of its original size mainly because of water diversion for agricultural usage in surrounding areas. Also, from a depth of 68 meters in the 1960s, today it stands at less than 10 meters. In Lake Baikal, the water level has gone below the critical mark of 456m eleven times since 1962. Thus, while the Aral Sea has dried up immensely, Lake Baikal shows more of a fluctuating regime and shrinkage is not comparable to that of the Aral Sea.

questions.q: Turkey is located between

options.C: Gulf of Suez and Mediterranean Sea
options.D: Gulf of Aqaba and Dead Sea

questions.explanation: Turkey is bounded on the north by the Black Sea and west by the Mediterranean Sea and the Aegean Sea, and on the northwest by Greece and Bulgaria.

questions.q: Which country has the largest coastline?
questions.explanation: Canada's coastline is the world's longest, measuring 243,042 km (including the mainland coast and the coasts of offshore islands). Coastal line lengths of other options are Australia (25,750km), the USA (19,924 km), and India (7,517 km). The Western and Eastern Ghats of India make the coastal line with the Indian Ocean with the former longer than the latter.

questions.q: The Pennines (Europe), the Appalachians (America) and the Aravallis (India) are examples of
questions.explanation: Pennines are uplands in England. They are said to be the backbone of England. They are examples of old mountains and folded mountains. Appalachians are also examples of old mountains and they are folded mountains. They are present in North America. Aravallis are also examples of old mountains and they are folded mountains. Block mountains also known as fault-block mountains are the result of faulting caused by the endogenetic forces of the earth. It is also known as horst mountain. ex; Black Forest Mountain, Sierra Nevada (USA), salt Range (Pakistan). Fold mountains are formed due to the folding of crustal rocks by compressive endogenetic forces. Aravallis and Appalachians are examples of old-fold mountains. Note: The Alps in Europe, the Andes of South America, and the Himalayas of India are examples of fold mountains.

questions.q: Match the rivers with the cities through which they are flowing and select the correct answer using the codes given below: City - River: A. Rotterdam 1. Seine; B. Paris 2. Potomac; C. Budapest 3. Rhine; D. Washington 4. Danube
questions.explanation: Seine River - Its drainage basin is in the Paris Basin. It flows through Paris and drains into the English Channel (a part of the North Atlantic Ocean). The Eiffel Tower is situated on its bank. Potomac River - It is a famous river in Washington, USA. It finally drains into the North Atlantic Ocean. The Rhine, a very famous river in Europe, flows through the rift valley. It originates from the Swiss Alps (Switzerland) and finally drains into the North Sea (a part of the North Atlantic Ocean) in Rotterdam. It crosses a total of 6 countries (including Germany, Austria, Netherlands etc) through its journey. Danube is the second-longest river in Europe, after the Volga in Russia. Budapest is situated on its bank. It finally drains into the black sea. It flows through 10 countries and the capital of 4 countries lies on the river namely Vienna, Bratislava, Budapest, and Belgrade. Note: The origin of the Danube River is the Black Forest Mountain (block mountain) of Germany.

questions.q: Consider the following pairs: River - Flows into: 1. Mekong - Andaman Sea; 2. Thames - Irish Sea; 3. Volga - Caspian Sea; 4. Zambezi - Indian Ocean. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Mekong - Originating in the icy headwaters of the Tibetan highlands, the Mekong River flows through the steep canyons of China, known as the upper basin, through lower basin countries Myanmar, Laos, Thailand, and Cambodia, before fanning across an expansive delta in Vietnam and emptying into the South China Sea. Thames - The River Thames is the longest river in England, flowing 215 miles from the Cotswolds to the North Sea. Volga - The Volga is the longest river in Europe, and its catchment area is almost entirely inside Russia. It belongs to the closed basin of the Caspian Sea, being the longest river to flow into a closed basin. Zambezi - It is the fourth-longest river in Africa, the longest east-flowing river in Africa and the largest flowing into the Indian Ocean from Africa. Hence 3 and 4 are correct.

questions.q: Which one of the following cities experiences an early sunset?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Prime meridian is 0-degree longitude. It passes through Greenwich of London. The further the east to it the sooner the sunrise. Now, if we analyse the relative position of these locations, we see that the latitude of Sydney is about 151 degrees East, Tokyo is 140 degrees East, Hong Kong is 114 degrees East and Singapore is about 104 degrees East. As Sydney lies easternmost to the Prime meridian in the Eastern Hemisphere, Sydney will experience the earliest sunrise and therefore also the earliest sunset.

questions.q: Afghanistan shares its largest international boundary with

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Afghanistan shares its boundaries with: China (76 km), Iran: 935 km, Pakistan: 2,430 km, Tajikistan: 1,206 km, Turkmenistan: 744 km. So, it shares the longest boundary with Pakistan and the shortest boundary with China. The boundary between Afghanistan and Pakistan is called the Durand line. India shares its shortest boundary with Afghanistan (106 km).

questions.q: The Khyber Pass connects which one of the following pairs of cities?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Khyber Pass connects Kabul in Afghanistan to Pakistan cities. It starts in Peshawar in Pakistan. Kabul is the capital of Afghanistan and Islamabad is the capital of Pakistan. Herat and Tehran are places in Iran. Iran shares its border with Pakistan among others.

questions.q: Which of the following are rivers?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Located on the equator, Lake Victoria is the second-largest freshwater lake in the world and the largest lake in Africa. It is the source of the White Nile River which constitutes around 35% of water in the Nile River. 'Murray-Darling' is the largest river system in Australia. Encounter Bay is formed at the confluence of the Murray-Darling River system, this belt is often called the Wheat basket of Australia. Erie and Huron are the two freshwater lakes of the Great Lakes system located in the USA and Canada.

questions.q: Hyderabad city is located in which of the following countries?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The City of Hyderabad is situated both in India and Pakistan. In India, it is the capital of Telangana state. In Pakistan, Hyderabad is the 2nd largest city in the Sindh region of Pakistan. India's Hyderabad stands on the banks of the Musi River.

questions.q: Which of the following countries is the leading producer of electronics?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: China, Hong Kong, and the U.S. are at the front of the pack in producing electronics on a global level. Maintaining its reputation for producing inexpensive consumer goods in large quantities, China contributes heavily to the electronics sector, as this is one of the country's most prominent export categories. China was the leading electronics exporter in 2018, commanding 33.8% of all electronics exports.

questions.q: On which coast is St. Petersburg city in Russia located?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: St. Petersburg city in Russia is located on the bank of the Baltic Sea. An abundance of beautiful canals which are the part of the Neva River that flows into the Baltic Sea, give it the nickname, 'the Venice of the North'.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a wine-producing province of France?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Burgundy, Alsace and Champagne are the famous wine-producing provinces of France while Sachsen is a wine-producing region of Germany.

questions.q: Coldwater current flowing along Namibian Coast is known as

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Benguela Current is a cold current that forms in the eastern part of the South Atlantic Ocean. Agulhas Current: It is a warm oceanic current that forms in the South-West Indian Ocean. Humboldt Current, also known as Peru Current, is a cold current. It is responsible for the aridity of Chile's Atacama Desert. Kuroshio Current: a hot current that is formed in the northwestern Pacific Ocean. It is also called Black Current or the Japan Current.

questions.q: The largest minority community in Iraq is

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Kurds are the largest ethnic minority of Iraq, representing the largest non-Arab ethnic minority. Arabs, Turkmen and Kurds represent the major ethnic groups in Iraq.

questions.q: Grasslands of the Brazilian Plateau are known as

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Temperate grasslands occur in mid-latitudes and in the interior of the continents. Tropical grasslands occur on both sides of the equator and spread up to the tropics. Veld - South Africa; Downs - Australia; Savanna - East Africa; Pampas - Argentina; Campos - Brazil; Prairie - North America; Llanos - Venezuela; Steppes - Central Asia.

questions.q: Which of the following rivers flows from India to Pakistan?

options.E: None of the Above/More Than One of The Above

questions.explanation: Rivers flowing from India to Pakistan are: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, and Sutlej. They are in the same order from north to south. Indus River: It is the longest and the most important river in Pakistan. Originating in the Tibetan plateau in the vicinity of Lake Mansarovar, it flows through the entire length of Pakistan and merges into the Arabian Sea near the city of Karachi. Jhelum: Known as Hydaspes in Greek it is of great historical importance. Srinagar is situated on its bank. Chenab: Also known as the Chandrabhaga River, it is formed by the combination of 2 tributaries Chandra and Bagha. These 2 emerge from the Lahaul and Spiti valleys of Himachal Pradesh.

questions.q: Which one of the following pairs of countries shares the longest mountain border?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The border length between Argentina and Chile is 5308km. Andes mountains extend throughout the border between these 2 countries, 5308 km could be taken as the length of the mountain border between Argentina and Chile. Total border length between: India and China = 3488 km; France and Spain = 656.3km; Afghanistan and Pakistan = 2640 km. Therefore, it could be easily concluded that the mountain border between Argentina and Chile is larger than that of all other borders mentioned above.

questions.q: Which one of the following pairs of territory and country is not correctly matched?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Reunion Island is a territory of France located in the Indian Ocean region. Macau is located on the Southern coast of China. It is a small, special administrative region (SAR) of China that operates under the 'One Country, Two Systems' principle. La Palma is an island in Spain. The Falkland Islands, also called Malvinas Islands or Spanish Islas Malvinas, is a self-governing overseas territory of the United Kingdom in the South Atlantic Ocean. All four pairs are correctly matched, hence E is the answer.

questions.q: What is the name of Tropical grassland in Venezuela?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Llanos are the tropical grasslands found in Venezuela.

questions.q: The grasslands are known by which name in the country that won the FIFA world cup 2022?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The FIFA World Cup 2022 was won by Argentina. The grasslands in Argentina are known as Pampas.

questions.q: Match the following shifting cultivation with the countries where they are practised and select the correct answer using the codes given below: Shifting Cultivation - Country: A. Jhumming 1. Malaysia; B. Milpa 2. Brazil; C. Roca 3. Mexico; D. Ladang 4. India

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The correct matching is: Jhumming - India (4); Milpa - Mexico (3); Roca - Brazil (2); Ladang - Malaysia (1). This corresponds to A-4, B-3, C-2, D-1, which does not match any of the given options (A, B, or C), so E (None of the above) is correct.

questions.q: Which regions are known as the 'Orchards of the world'?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Mediterranean regions are known as the 'Orchards of the world' due to the ideal conditions for fruit production.

questions.q: What is the main mineral constituent of the oceanic crust?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D (A and C) - both Silica and Magnesium are the main mineral constituents of the oceanic crust. This is why the oceanic crust is referred to as SIMA (Silica and Magnesium).

questions.q: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer from the codes given below the lists: List-I: a. Loss  b. Moraines  c. Dunes  d. Mushroom rocks  |  List-II: 1. Rocks have a narrower base and wider top  2. Fine sand is deposited by the action of the wind  3. Debris of boulders and coarse material carried by glaciers  4. A small hill of sand caused by the action of the wind

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Loss - Fine sand is deposited by the action of the wind (2); Moraines - Debris of boulders and coarse material carried by glaciers (3); Dunes - A small hill of sand caused by the action of the wind (4); Mushroom rocks - Rocks have a narrower base and wider top (1). Hence the correct match is a-2, b-3, c-4, d-1.

questions.q: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer from the codes given below the lists: List-I: a. North America  b. South America  c. Africa  d. Australia  |  List-II: 1. Andes mountain  2. Great Sandy desert  3. Drakensberg mountain  4. Appalachian mountain

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: North America - Appalachian mountain (4); South America - Andes mountain (1); Africa - Drakensberg mountain (3); Australia - Great Sandy desert (2). Hence the correct match is a-4, b-1, c-3, d-2.

questions.q: Prince Charles mountains are present in which continent?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Prince Charles mountains are present in Antarctica.

questions.q: Greenland is a part of

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Greenland is geographically located in North America but politically it is a part of Denmark (Europe).

questions.q: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer from the codes given below the lists: List-I: a. Urmia Lake  b. Irrawaddy River  c. Red River  d. Mekong River  |  List-II: 1. Myanmar  2. Laos  3. Vietnam  4. Iran

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Urmia Lake - Iran (4); Irrawaddy River - Myanmar (1); Red River - Vietnam (3); Mekong River - Laos (2). Hence the correct match is a-4, b-1, c-3, d-2.
